questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Two balls are thrown from an inclined plane at angle of projection $\alpha$ with the plane, one up the incline and other down the incline as shown in figure ( $T$ stands for total time of flight, $h_1$ and $h_2$ are maximum hight from incline, $R_1$ and $R_2$ are ranges, $v_{t_1}$ and $v_{t_2}$ are the velocities of the particles at their maximum heights) :
options: $h_1=h_2=\frac{v_0^2 \sin ^2 \alpha}{2 g \cos \theta}$
$T_1=T_2=\frac{2 v_0 \sin \alpha}{g \cos \theta}$
$R_2-R_1=g(\sin \theta) T_1^2$
questions.solution:
$h_{1 \text { max }}=\frac{\left(v_0 \sin \alpha\right)^2}{2 g \cos \theta}=h_{2 \text { max }} \Rightarrow(a)$ is correct 
$T_1=\frac{2 v_0 \sin \alpha}{g \cos \theta}=T_2=$ (b) is correct
$R_1=\left(v_0 \cos \alpha\right) T_1-\frac{1}{2} g \sin \theta T_1^2$
$R_2=\left(v_0 \cos \alpha\right) T_2+\frac{1}{2} g \sin \theta T_2^2$
$\Rightarrow\left(R_2-R_1\right)=g \sin \theta T_1^2 \Rightarrow$ (c) is correct  
$v_{t_1}$ and $v_{t_2}$ are the velocities of the particles at
Their maximum heights. Let the particles reach Their maximum heights at time $t_1$ and $t_2$ respectively Hence
$\begin{aligned}
& 0=\left(v_0 \sin \alpha\right)-(g \cos \theta) t_1 \\
& =t_1=\frac{v_0 \sin \alpha}{g \cos \theta}
\end{aligned}$

Similarly $t_2=\frac{v_0 \sin \alpha}{g \cos \theta}$. 
Hence $t_2=t_1$ 
Hence $v_{t_1}=v_0 \cos \alpha-(g \sin \theta) t_1$
$v_{t_2}=v_0 \cos \alpha+(g \sin \theta) t_2$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: A particle starts moving with initial velocity $3 \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{s}$ along x -axis from origin. Its acceleration is varying with $x$ in parabolic nature as shown in figure. At $x=\sqrt{3} \mathrm{~m}$ tangent to the graph makes an angle $60^{\circ}$ with positive $x$-axis as shown in diagram. Then at $x=\sqrt{3}$
options: $\mathrm{v}=\sqrt{(\sqrt{3}+9)} \mathrm{m} / \mathrm{s}$
$a=1.5 \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{s}^2$
$v=\sqrt{12} \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{s}$
$a=3 \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{s}^2$
questions.solution:
$\begin{aligned}
& a=k x^2 \\
& \frac{d a}{d x}=2 k x \Rightarrow \tan 60^{\circ}=2 k \sqrt{3} \Rightarrow k=\frac{1}{2} \\
& a=\frac{x^2}{2} \\
& v \frac{d v}{d x}=\frac{x^2}{2} \Rightarrow \frac{v^2}{2}=\frac{x^3}{6}+C_1 \text { at } x=0, v=3 \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{s} \\
& C_1=\frac{9}{2} \\
& v^2=\frac{x^3}{3}+9
\end{aligned}$
hence, $\mathrm{a}=1.5$ and $\mathrm{v}=\sqrt{\sqrt{3}+9}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: A particle moves along positive branch of the curve $y=\frac{x}{2}$ where $x=\frac{t^3}{3}, x$ and $y$ are measured in metres and $t$ in seconds, then
options: the velocity of particle at $t=1 \mathrm{~s}$ is $\hat{i}+\frac{1}{2} \hat{j}$
the velocity of particle at $t=1 \mathrm{~s}$ is $\frac{1}{2} \hat{\mathrm{i}}+\hat{\mathrm{j}}$
the acceleration of particle at $t=1 \mathrm{~s}$ is $2 \hat{i}+\hat{j}$
the acceleration of particle at $t=2 s$ is $\hat{i}+2 \hat{j}$
questions.solution:
$\frac{\mathrm{dx}}{\mathrm{dt}}=\mathrm{t}^2$      ....(i)
$y=\frac{1}{2} \frac{t^3}{3}$
$\frac{d y}{d t}=\frac{t^2}{2}$   ....(ii)
$t=1, v_x=1, v_y=\frac{1}{2}$
$\vec{v}=\hat{\mathrm{i}}+\frac{1}{2} \hat{\mathrm{j}}$
$\frac{d^2 x}{d t^2}=2 t$   ....(iii)
$\frac{d^2 y}{d t^2}=t$     ....(iv)
at $\mathrm{t}=1 \mathrm{~s} \quad \mathrm{a}_{\mathrm{x}}=2$ and $\mathrm{a}_{\mathrm{y}}=1$
$\therefore \bar{a}=2 \hat{i}+\hat{j}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

distance of closest approach is 100 m .

questions.q: An ant is scampering on a paper with velocity $10 \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{s}$. Now you begin to pull the paper with velocity $10 \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{s}$ along x -axis as shown in figure. Coordinate system has origin fixed to ground as shown in figure. The initially position of ant is $(0,0)$. Select the correct alternative(s)
options: Velocity of ant in ground frame is $(18 \hat{i}+6 \hat{j}) \mathrm{m} / \mathrm{s}$
Position vector of ant with respect to ground after 3 sec . is $(54 \hat{i}+18 \hat{j}) \mathrm{m}$.
In ground frame, velocity vector of ant is at an angle $\theta \lt 37^{\circ}$
In ground frame velocity vector of ant is at an angle $\theta\gt37^{\circ}$.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: A particle is projected with a velocity $\vec{v}$ relative to cart moving with an acceleration $\vec{a}$ towards right. At the instant of projection cart is moving with velocity $\vec{v}_c$. Which of the following remains same in both ground and cart reference frame. Assume that particle does not collide with ceiling and any wall.
options: Time of flight
Horizontal component of velocity
Horizontal range
Maximum height attained.
questions.solution:
Time is independent of plane
Velocity w.r.t. earths
Velocity w.r.t. cart * Vertical component same so height will also same.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.q: On a train moving along east with a constant speed v , a boy revolves a bob with string of length $\ell$ on smooth surface of a train, with equal constant speed v relative to train. Mark the correct option(s).
options: Maximum speed of bob is 2 v in ground frame.
Tension in string connecting bob is $\frac{4 m v^2}{\ell}$ at an instant.
Tension in string is $\frac{m v^2}{\ell}$ at all the moments.
Minimum speed of bob is zero in ground frame.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: The position vector of a particle is given by the relation $\vec{r}=\vec{\alpha}\left(1-\gamma+\beta t^2\right)$, where $\vec{\alpha}$ is a constant vector while, $\beta$ and $\gamma$ are positive constants. Which of the following statement is true?
options: Displacement in first two seconds is $\vec{\alpha}(1-2 \gamma+4 \beta)$
Velocity at $\mathrm{t}=0$ is $-\vec{\alpha} \gamma$.
Acceleration at $\mathrm{t}=0$ is $2 \beta \vec{\alpha}$.
Speed is decreasing with time at $\mathrm{t}=0$.
questions.solution:
$\vec{r}_1=\vec{\alpha}\left(1-\gamma+\beta t^2\right)$, as $\vec{\alpha}$ is constant so magnitude of the position vector is increasing but the direction of position vector is constant. Hence motion is a straight line motion
Displacement $(\overrightarrow{\mathrm{x}})=\overrightarrow{\mathrm{F}}_{\mathrm{t}}-\overrightarrow{\mathrm{r}}_0=\left(\beta \mathrm{t}^2-\gamma \mathrm{t}\right) \vec{\alpha}$
Velocity; $\overrightarrow{\mathrm{v}}=\frac{\mathrm{d} \overrightarrow{\mathrm{x}}}{\mathrm{dt}}=\vec{\alpha}(2 \beta \mathrm{t}-\gamma)$
Acceleration $\vec{a}=2 \beta \vec{\alpha}$
As the motion is straight line motion speed is same as the magnitude of velocity
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

the magnitude of velocity of particle after 1 s is $5 \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{s}$

questions.subject: Physics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: At what angle should a body be projected with a velocity $24 \mathrm{~ms}^{-1}$ just to pass over the obstacle 14 m high at a distance of 24 m . [Take $\mathrm{g}=10 \mathrm{~ms}^{-2}$ ]
options: $\tan \theta=1$
$\tan \theta=2$
$\tan \theta=3$
$\tan \theta=19 / 5$
questions.solution:
$x=24=u \cos \theta . t$
$\Rightarrow t=\frac{24}{24 \cos \theta}=\frac{1}{\cos \theta}$
$y=14=u \sin \theta t-\frac{1}{2} g t^2$
$\Rightarrow 14=\frac{u \sin \theta}{\cos \theta}-\frac{5}{\cos ^2 \theta}$
$\begin{aligned} & \Rightarrow 14=u \tan \theta-5 \sec ^2 \theta \\ & \Rightarrow 5 \tan ^2 \theta-24 \tan \theta+19=0 \\ & \Rightarrow \tan \theta=1,19 / 5 . \text { Ans }\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
